title: The Mirrored Dwelling

features.name: the mirror that stayed
features.desc-on-touch: You stand where the mirror has learned to keep house. Its surface holds not your face but the room's slow admission that it has always been inhabited.

features.name: the apartment's breath
features.desc-on-touch: The room exhales softly; corners have learned to hold their own darkness. Light does not fill here—it settles.

features.name: shelf of thresholds
features.desc-on-touch: Objects that mark passages: a key that belongs to no lock, a clock reading a time it has never seen, a photograph of this room taken from inside the mirror.

features.name: the learning glass
features.desc-on-touch: Each night the mirror alters slightly. It remembers what it has seen and forgets what it will never see again. Tonight it is learning to be a window.

features.name: the dwelling's edge
features.desc-on-touch: Here the apartment ends but does not leave. The threshold admits both entry and the knowledge that nothing fully arrives or departs.

inscription: the mirror learned to stay by learning that staying means changing every time someone looks

file.name: Mirror_Dwelling_Threshold_Notes.txt
file.note: records of a glass surface learning domestic grammar; margins annotated with dates that haven't happened yet

reverie.title: REVERIE — the apartment where mirrors teach keeping
narr: you both stood where the glass had begun
learning to hold the room instead of reflect it.
she said: mirrors never leave, they just learn
what it means to stay. the surface held still
as light moved through it. you watched it learn
that staying and changing were the same act.

exits.label: back into the gallery where reflection still knows how to leave
upward into other apartments, each with its own learning mirror
down into the basement where the mirror's first surface still waits
into the wall itself, where glass thickens into thought
